programme.title: California's Gold
programme.desc: Naturalist and conservationist John Muir founded the Sierra Club in 1892 to protect Yosemite National Park.

programme.title: California's Golden Parks
programme.desc: Visiting a different California state park each month.

programme.title: PBS News Hour
programme.desc: Co-anchors Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett and correspondents offer in-depth analysis of current events.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Mapping the roots of actress Kate Burton and media mogul Barry Diller.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Actors Ted Danson, Mary Steenburgen and William H. Macy trace their nonconformist ancestors back to the Puritan establishment.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: Congress puts George Washington in command of a new Continental Army; Congress declares American independence in July 1776.

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates: The American Revolution
programme.desc: Exploring how the American Revolution affected Britain, with sabotage, espionage and political unrest reshaping the empire.

programme.title: PBS News Hour
programme.desc: Co-anchors Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett and correspondents offer in-depth analysis of current events.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Actors Ted Danson, Mary Steenburgen and William H. Macy trace their nonconformist ancestors back to the Puritan establishment.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: Congress puts George Washington in command of a new Continental Army; Congress declares American independence in July 1776.

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates: The American Revolution
programme.desc: Exploring how the American Revolution affected Britain, with sabotage, espionage and political unrest reshaping the empire.

programme.title: Nature Cat
programme.sub-title: The Nature Nap Dilemma; Captain Nature Cat's Wild River Adventure
programme.desc: The gang goes looking for their Nature Nap hammock; Nature Cat and his pals help an oyster named Elroy get back home.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop
programme.desc: The Loops celebrate the day they met Stu; Liana finds crumbs on Louisa's pillow and accuses her of taking the last cookie.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.sub-title: Speak Up Francine; Waiting for Snow
programme.desc: Francine must overcome her fear of public speaking so she can voice her concern about pollution at the Earth Day rally; Ladonna dreams of skiing and sledding.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.sub-title: Breakfast Club; Dr. O: Party Time, Excellent
programme.desc: Delivery Debbie goes undercover to stop a group of villains; when Olympia finds out Dr. O is leaving, she struggles to throw a great goodbye party.

programme.title: Wild Kratts: Our Blue and Green World
programme.desc: While doing their annual Laundry Day, the Kratt brothers disagree on what is better -- blue oceans or green forests.

programme.title: Nature Cat: Nature Cat's Nature Movie Special Extraordinaire
programme.desc: Nature Cat and pals realize they've come to the end of their Nature Curiosity List; Sir Galahad gives himself the title of King, moves into a castle and steals pieces of nature to make it look more beautiful.

programme.title: Wild Kratts: Cats and Dogs
programme.desc: Martin and Chris observe wild cats and dogs interacting with each other on the African savanna.

programme.title: Sesame Street
programme.desc: Elmo, Grover, and all their friends help Charlie build a Little Library for her Mitzvah Day project for her synagogue. Helping neighbors share books is Charlie's way of making the world a kinder place.

programme.title: Work It Out Wombats!
programme.sub-title: Ellie's Island; Zadie & Buckley's Playdate Problem
programme.desc: Ellie's so excited to be going home to Jamaica that the Wombats and Mr. E start to worry whether she'll come back; Buckley comes over for a playdate with Zadie.

programme.desc: While doing their annual Laundry Day, the Kratt brothers disagree on what is better -- blue oceans or green forests.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Neil Armstrong-signed Apollo 11 moon landing photos; a Tongan war club made around 1800; 1993 "Magic: The Gathering" beta cards.

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Mike, Kristin, and the team look into the murder of a farmer's market owner, who staggers into the crowd with a garden implement embedded into her back.

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Part 2/2. Foyle's investigation of a body on a deserted beach poses a threat to American aid donations.

programme.title: PBS News Hour
programme.desc: Co-anchors Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett and correspondents offer in-depth analysis of current events.

programme.title: Life Unearthed With Ariel Waldman
programme.desc: Following Ariel Waldman's solo expedition through Antarctica's extreme environments as she documents the thriving microscopic wildlife beneath the ice.

programme.title: Nature
programme.desc: Tiger expert Valmik Thapar uncovers 50 years of archival footage chronicling the tiger clan of northwestern India's Ranthambore National Park.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: NOVA
programme.desc: Scientists try to figure out how to predict weather events called "rain bombs" that sink ships and down planes.

programme.title: Wilding
programme.desc: Isabella Tree and her husband, Charlie Burrell, strive to breathe new life into the ailing Knepp estate. They reintroduce native ponies, deer, pigs and other wildlife, allowing nature to thrive.

programme.desc: Dot and Al announce the Hunters are spending Christmas in Nigeria, and Corky wonders if she can have a fun Christmas without snow.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop
programme.sub-title: Lyla Can't Sleep; Hairdos and Hair Don'ts
programme.desc: Lyla struggles to sleep and tries to figure out what is wrong with her sleep routine; Lyla tries to do her own hairstyle.

programme.title: Carl the Collector
programme.sub-title: The Fake Mustache Collection; The Lint Dinosaur Collection
programme.desc: Nico is tired of being mistaken for her twin sister; Carl is upset when Nico breaks the Lint Dinosaur Monday rules and makes a Lint Monster instead.

programme.title: Sesame Street
programme.desc: Cinderella needs to tell time for the ball, but her clock is broken, so Nina, Abby and Rudy wonder what tool they can use to fix it.

programme.title: Work It Out Wombats!
programme.sub-title: A Room of Our Own; Zeke's Fairy Village
programme.desc: Malik and Zadie can't agree on how tidy to keep their bedroom; Zeke's friends build houses for the fairies who have moved in.

programme.title: Donkey Hodie
programme.sub-title: Acornball; Super Duper Magic Fun Box
programme.desc: Donkey and Panda make up a game while waiting for Gameshow Gator's new game; Donkey and Panda disagree on which toys are fun.

programme.title: Rosie's Rules
programme.sub-title: An Anniversary Tradition; Chiles en Nogada Day
programme.desc: Rosie, Crystal and Iggy try to recreate Mom and Papá's beach anniversary tradition at home; it's Chiles en Nogada Day, but when Papá loses the recipe, it's up to Rosie to figure out who else might have it.

programme.title: Elinor Wonders Why
programme.desc: Elinor finds a peculiar-looking owl while visiting her grandparents in the desert; Olive and her friends investigate why a tree has fallen over in the forest.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A Hassan el Glaoui tempera painting circa 1970; Line Vautrin Talosel mirror; a diamond horseshoe brooch circa 1900.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When one of the world's rarest orchids is smuggled illegally into Midsomer Malham, it triggers a catalogue of passion, jealousy and death.

programme.title: The Forsytes on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Disaster strikes at Forsyte & Co. as Soames puts an end to Irene's Parisian dreams; Louisa faces a shock eviction.
